summary: Audit-only inspection of test impact for changing the admin Notion editor autosave cadence to about 1 second and adding Ctrl+S/Meta+S manual save.
goals: Identify existing Vitest coverage to modify or extend
Identify existing Playwright coverage to modify or extend
Call out brittle selectors and timing assumptions

implementationAnchors.detail: Autosave is scheduled by a timeout-based effect.
Full blog editor already has a Ctrl+S/Meta+S keyboard save pattern.

actions: Replace 9500ms/500ms timer assumptions with roughly 900-950ms then threshold-crossing assertions for a 1s debounce.
Assert no PUT before the threshold and one PUT after it.
Add a keyboard-save test that dispatches ctrl/meta+s, verifies preventDefault, fires an immediate save, and does not double-save after timers advance.
Rename the first autosave test so it no longer encodes a long autosave wait.
Reduce 15000ms save-state waits to a tighter 3000-5000ms range aligned with a 1s debounce plus CI slack.
Match the autosave PUT by request body containing the edited text.
Add a Ctrl+S/Meta+S manual-save test that proves save happens before the autosave timer would have expired.
Replace fixed sleep inference in the throttled revalidation test with request-count based assertions.
After switching documents and editing, assert the Notion save chip reaches Saved quickly.
Optionally add a keyboard-save branch to ensure the shortcut applies to the active document after switching.
Extend visual-state coverage to include the Saving... chip state.
Exercise at least one branch through the keyboard-save path so manual save shares the same chip styling contract.

brittleAssumptions.detail: Uses .tiptap.ProseMirror instead of a stable editor-specific test surface.
Uses waitForTimeout(900) to manufacture a visible Saving state.
Uses waitForTimeout(1500) as a proxy for no second revalidation.
Hard-codes the old 10-second debounce via fake timers.
Character-by-character typing can race with a 1-second debounce in slower CI runs.

validations: Ran repo-wide ripgrep searches for Notion/autosave/save-state/keyboard-save coverage
risks: Manual save semantics are not yet defined for dirty metadata fields in Notion view.
A 1-second debounce increases flake risk for tests that type long strings character-by-character.
Saving... may become too transient for naive visibility assertions.
recommendation: Update the unit test and the dedicated Notion autosave Playwright spec first, then extend visual-state coverage for the keyboard-save path while reducing fixed sleeps and class-based editor selectors.
